auth.unauthorized: You do not have permission to access this resource

classificationSelector.label: Classification level
classificationSelector.helpText: Controls who may view this evidence.
classificationSelector.sideRequiredError: An ex parte side is required when classification is ex parte.

legalHoldControl.label: Legal hold
legalHoldControl.notificationHelp: The reason you enter below will be included in the notification delivered to all case members. It is not stored as a separate audit field — the custody chain records the toggle itself.
legalHoldControl.notificationPlaceText: This evidence has been placed on legal hold. Deletion and destruction are suspended until the hold is released.
legalHoldControl.notificationReleaseText: The legal hold on this evidence has been released. Standard retention rules now apply.
legalHoldControl.warningPlace: While on hold, this evidence cannot be destroyed or have its retention shortened.
legalHoldControl.warningRelease: Releasing the hold restores normal retention and destruction rules.

retentionDatePicker.label: Retention until
retentionDatePicker.pastHint: The retention period has already expired.
retentionDatePicker.helpText: Date after which this evidence is eligible for destruction.

destructionDialog.title: Destroy evidence
destructionDialog.step1Body: Destruction removes the file bytes permanently. The evidence number, SHA-256 hash, metadata, and chain-of-custody history will be preserved for legal integrity. The raw file cannot be recovered.
destructionDialog.step2Body: Enter the court order reference or legal basis authorising this destruction. This will be recorded immutably in the custody chain.
destructionDialog.step2MinLengthError: Authority must be at least 10 characters.
destructionDialog.step3Body: You are about to destroy the following evidence. Review carefully — this cannot be undone.
destructionDialog.step4Body: The file has been destroyed. The following records remain intact:
destructionDialog.step4ItemCustody: Complete chain of custody with destruction entry

tagInput.invalidChars: Tags may contain letters, numbers, hyphens and underscores only.
tagInput.tooLong: Tag must be 100 characters or fewer.

integrity.receiptExplanation: This is the cryptographic fingerprint of your file. Save this value — you can verify it later against the server-stored hash to confirm nothing was altered in transit.
integrity.mismatchBody: The file that reached our server does not match the file on your device. This is usually caused by a flaky connection or antivirus interference. Your original file is untouched.

captureValidation.invalidSourceUrl: Source URL must use http or https.
captureValidation.invalidProfileUrl: Profile URL must use http or https.
captureValidation.geoPairRequired: Both latitude and longitude are required.
captureValidation.publicationAfterCapture: Publication date is after capture date.
captureValidation.verifiedMetadataLocked: Verified metadata cannot be modified without elevated role.

search.title: Search Evidence
search.noResultsHint: No evidence matching "{query}" was found. Try different keywords or adjust your filters.
search.subtitle: Full-text search across indexed evidence. Typos tolerated, filters refine.
search.idleHint: Query by keyword, or refine with filters. Supports highlighting, facets, and typo tolerance.

errors.networkError: Unable to connect to the server

hero.title: Where custody, provenance, and disclosure are beyond question.
hero.description: VaultKeeper gives human rights documentation teams and legal case builders authoritative control over evidence workflows — from secure intake and chain-of-custody tracking to court-ready disclosure packages. Supports deployment in your jurisdiction, on your infrastructure.

berkeleyProtocol.title: Berkeley Protocol-aligned
berkeleyProtocol.description: Evidence handling workflows informed by the Berkeley Protocol for digital open-source investigations.

rfc3161.title: RFC 3161 trusted timestamps
rfc3161.description: Cryptographic proof of when evidence was sealed via RFC 3161 trusted timestamping.

sovereignty.title: Sovereign deployment
sovereignty.description: Deploy in your own jurisdiction and infrastructure, with sovereign hosting options that reduce third-party data exposure.

auditTrails.title: Immutable audit trails
auditTrails.description: Every access, transfer, and modification recorded. Tamper-evident chain of custody from intake to courtroom.

openSource.title: Built in the open
openSource.description: VaultKeeper is AGPL-3.0 licensed. Every line of code can be inspected, audited, and self-hosted. For organizations that cannot afford black-box trust assumptions, transparency is the foundation.
openSource.license: AGPL-3.0 — Free to inspect, audit, and self-host

features.title: Every link in the chain, accounted for
features.subtitle: From the moment evidence enters the system to the moment it’s presented in court — VaultKeeper ensures nothing is lost, altered, or questioned.

intake.title: Secure Evidence Intake
intake.description: Upload evidence with automated SHA-256 hash verification and RFC 3161 trusted timestamping. Every file is integrity-sealed from the moment it enters the system.

custody.title: Chain of Custody
custody.description: Every access, transfer, modification, and export is recorded in an immutable audit trail. Full provenance from intake to courtroom.

access.title: Role-Based Access Control
access.description: Granular permissions by case, role, and classification level. Investigators, prosecutors, and defence counsel see only what they’re authorized to see.

disclosure.title: Disclosure Management
disclosure.description: Structured workflows for prosecution and defence disclosure obligations. Generate compliant packages with complete chain-of-custody documentation.

redaction.title: Collaborative Redaction
redaction.description: Real-time collaborative PDF redaction with version control. Multiple reviewers can work simultaneously with live presence indicators.

search.title: Search & Intelligence
search.description: Full-text search across all evidence with advanced filtering by case, classification, date range, and file type. Find what matters, fast.

howItWorks.title: From intake to courtroom in four steps

ingest.title: Ingest
ingest.description: Upload evidence with automated integrity verification and trusted timestamping.

organize.title: Organize
organize.description: Classify, tag, and assign to cases with full audit trails and role-based access.

collaborate.title: Collaborate
collaborate.description: Review, redact, and prepare evidence with real-time collaborative tools.

disclose.title: Disclose
disclose.description: Generate court-ready disclosure packages with complete chain-of-custody proof.

pricing.title: Plans that scale with your caseload
pricing.subtitle: Start with the pilot program at no cost. Scale to enterprise-grade sovereign evidence management as your needs grow.

pilot.name: Pilot
pilot.description: For teams evaluating sovereign evidence management.

professional.name: Professional
professional.description: For documentation and legal teams with active caseloads.

sovereignty.question: Where is my data stored?
sovereignty.answer: VaultKeeper supports sovereign deployment options. Your evidence data can be hosted in your jurisdiction of choice — on-premises, in a private cloud, or in a region-locked managed environment. You retain full control over data residency.

security.question: What security certifications does VaultKeeper hold?
security.answer: VaultKeeper is built for ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II compliance, with GDPR compliance by design. All data is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3). Certification roadmap available on request.

integration.question: Can VaultKeeper integrate with our existing systems?
integration.answer: Yes. VaultKeeper provides a comprehensive REST API for integration with case management systems, court filing platforms, and forensic tools. SSO integration via SAML 2.0 and OpenID Connect is supported.

pilot.question: What does the pilot program include?
pilot.answer: The pilot program gives your team access to core VaultKeeper features at no cost for 90 days. This includes evidence intake, chain-of-custody tracking, role-based access, and basic search. Our team will help you onboard and evaluate the platform.

pricing.question: How does pricing work?
pricing.answer: Professional plans are priced per user per month. Enterprise plans are custom-quoted based on deployment model, storage requirements, and support needs. Contact our sales team for a detailed quote tailored to your organization.

languages.question: Does VaultKeeper support multiple languages?
languages.answer: Yes. VaultKeeper currently supports English and French, with additional languages on the roadmap. The interface, notifications, and exports can all be configured per user locale.

training.question: What training and onboarding is available?
training.answer: All plans include documentation and video tutorials. Professional plans include guided onboarding sessions. Enterprise plans include custom training programs, dedicated onboarding specialists, and ongoing support.

migration.question: Can we migrate data from our existing evidence system?
migration.answer: Yes. VaultKeeper supports bulk evidence import with automated hash verification to ensure integrity during migration. Our team provides migration planning and execution support for Professional and Enterprise customers.

cta.title: Ready to secure your evidence workflows?
cta.description: Sovereign evidence management for human rights documentation and legal case-building. Start with a free pilot — no commitment required.

footer.description: Sovereign evidence management for legal and criminal investigations. Secure intake, chain-of-custody, and court-ready disclosure.

featuresPage.title: Everything you need to manage evidence with authority
featuresPage.description: A complete toolkit for secure evidence intake, custody tracking, collaborative review, and court-ready disclosure.

pricingPage.title: Transparent pricing for every team size
pricingPage.description: Start free with the pilot program. Scale seamlessly as your investigation needs grow.

aboutPage.title: Evidence integrity is the foundation of justice.
aboutPage.description: VaultKeeper was built from a single conviction: that the systems handling criminal evidence must be as rigorous, transparent, and trustworthy as the legal processes they serve. We build tools that investigators, prosecutors, and defence counsel can rely on — under oath, under scrutiny, and under pressure.
aboutPage.mission: Every piece of evidence tells a story. Our job is to ensure that story reaches the courtroom exactly as it happened — unaltered, unquestioned, and complete.

sovereignty.title: Data Sovereignty
sovereignty.description: Your evidence, your jurisdiction. VaultKeeper can be deployed on-premises, in a private cloud, or in a region-locked managed environment. You choose where your data lives.

transparency.title: Radical Transparency
transparency.description: Every access, transfer, and modification is recorded in an immutable audit trail. Nothing happens to your evidence without a clear, verifiable record.

integrity.title: Uncompromising Integrity
integrity.description: SHA-256 hash verification and RFC 3161 trusted timestamps ensure that evidence integrity can be proven at any point — from intake to courtroom presentation.

security.title: Security by Design
security.description: End-to-end encryption, role-based access control, and regular third-party audits. Security is not a feature — it’s the foundation everything else is built on.

contact.title: Register for pilot access
contact.description: Join the VaultKeeper pilot program and experience sovereign evidence management firsthand. Our team will work with you to evaluate the platform for your specific needs.

response.title: Response within 24 hours
response.description: Our team reviews every application personally.

security.title: Your data is secure
security.description: Handled with the same care we bring to evidence.

email.description: For urgent inquiries, reach us directly.
